edges.desc: The Sword Coast region contains the city of Neverwinter
The Forge of Spells is located within Wave Echo Cave
Phandalin prospered alongside the dwarves and gnomes who mined Wave Echo Cave
Gundren Rockseeker travels with Sildar Hallwinter as his escort
The Black Spider has Gundren Rockseeker captured and his map taken
Sildar tells the characters about the lost mine and that Gundren was taken to Cragmaw Castle
The Black Spider wants the adventurers out of the picture and is their final adversary in Wave Echo Cave
The adventurers are friends with Gundren Rockseeker and seek to rescue him
The characters rescue Sildar Hallwinter from the goblins
Players can invent their own reasons for visiting Phandalin or use provided adventure hooks
hires the players in Neverwinter to escort a wagon to Phandalin
is present in Neverwinter when hiring the players
goes ahead to attend to business in Phandalin
accompanies Gundren Rockseeker to Phandalin
are in Neverwinter when hired by Gundren Rockseeker
are tasked with delivering a wagon to Phandalin
will pay the players 10 gp each upon safe delivery of the wagon
Players travel through the Sword Coast region, delving into ancient strongholds and exploring ruins
Knights (as player characters) dare the crypts of the fallen dwarf kings of Delzoun seeking glory and treasure
Wizards (as player characters) plunder the ruins of the fallen Netherese empire
Rogues (as player characters) prowl the dark alleyways of Baldur's Gate
Players are escorting Gundren Rockseeker's wagon of supplies as hired help
Players travel south along the High Road
Players travel east along the Triboar Trail
Players encounter goblin raiders at the ambush site
was in Neverwinter when departing with Gundren
Four goblins are hiding in the woods at the ambush site, waiting to attack
Goblins ambush the players when they approach the dead horses, with two making melee attacks and two making ranged attacks
Four goblins work together in the ambush, coordinating their attacks
Goblins are positioned at the ambush site, with two on each side of the road
Goblins guard the ambush site and attack anyone who investigates the dead horses
Goblins are hiding in the woods at the ambush site, using Stealth to surprise the players
defeated the goblins at the ambush site
pursues the goblins to their hideout
The Cragmaw tribe has orders to waylay Gundren Rockseeker and anyone traveling with him
The players have arrived at the cave entrance following the goblins' trail
Two goblins are stationed at a guard post, watching for intruders
Three wolves are confined in the kennel at Cragmaw Hideout
Wolves attack any creature except goblins that moves into the room
Players can attempt to calm the wolves with a Wisdom (Animal Handling) check, with DC reduced if food is given
A character attempting to ascend or descend the chimney shaft in the location
One goblin stands watch on the bridge guard post in the hideout
The players are currently exploring the old streambed, hearing a waterfall ahead
The goblins have been beating and tormenting Sildar Hallwinter
Yeemik (goblin leader) grabs Sildar and threatens to kill him if characters don't agree to truce
Yeemik tries to convince the adventurers to kill Klarg, promising to release Sildar when they bring back the bugbear's head
met in Neverwinter and agreed to accompany him back to Phandalin
one of the three Rockseeker brothers who located Wave Echo Cave
present at the goblin ambush site where he provides information to the players
remains with the party if rescued and healed, joining them as an NPC party member
Gundren Rockseeker's gear was taken to Cragmaw Castle, indicating he was previously at Cragmaw Hideout
Klarg the bugbear shares this cave with his pet wolf and goblins
Klarg the bugbear has a pet wolf named Ripper that he controls
Klarg commands the goblins under him, though they resent his bullying
The central fire pit in cragmaw_hideout deals 1 fire damage to any creature that enters it, or 1d6 fire damage to any creature that falls prone there
The characters want to seek out Cragmaw Castle to find and rescue Gundren Rockseeker
The party's goal is to find and rescue Gundren Rockseeker
Gundren Rockseeker is believed to be at Cragmaw Castle and needs rescue
The townmaster is looking for someone (players) to run off orc raiders seen on the east end of Triboar Trail
Characters can purchase adventuring gear (except weapons/armor and items over 25 gp) at Barthen's Provisions
Barthen pays the players 10 gp each for delivering supplies and encourages them to rescue Gundren Rockseeker
Barthen considers Gundren a friend and was excited about his talk of discovering the lost mine
Barthen encourages the party to find and rescue Gundren Rockseeker after learning of his capture
Daran asks the characters to visit the ruins and find out who's there and what they're up to
The party investigates Old Owl Well
The players are directed to Tresendar Manor to find the Redbrand base
Halia offers the players 100 gp to eliminate Glasstaff and bring her correspondence from his quarters
Carp offers to take the characters to the secret tunnel or provide directions to Tresendar Manor where bandits have a secret lair
Qelline suggests the players visit Reidoth at Thundertree ruins to get help finding specific sites like Cragmaw Castle or Wave Echo Cave
Sister Garaele offers the quest to the characters to persuade Agatha the banshee in exchange for three potions of healing
Town notice posted at townmaster's hall offers reward for dealing with orcs near Wyvern Tor
Harbin directs the party to Wyvern Tor to deal with the orc band causing trouble
encourages the characters to keep up the pressure on the Cragmaw goblins
suggests the party might find Cragmaw Castle by searching the lands around the Triboar Trail for more raiding parties
Sildar wants Iarno captured and transported to Neverwinter to face judgment
party eliminates the goblin threat from Cragmaw Castle
If the characters investigate this place, they find the entrance to the Redbrand hideout
The characters may decide to investigate and confront the Redbrands at Tresendar Manor
Redbrands attack the players after insults and baiting if the players don't attack first
Redbrands are present in Phandalin, specifically at the Sleeping Giant and in the streets
party defeats the Redbrand Ruffians in Phandalin, earning 400 XP divided equally among characters
can be used to pick the locked door in the hideout under Tresendar Manor
contained in a waterproof satchel hidden in the cistern at Tresendar Manor as part of Iarno's getaway kit
contained in a waterproof satchel hidden in the cistern at Tresendar Manor as part of Iarno's getaway kit
Three Redbrand ruffians are resting in this room
three skeletons are animated and present in the mausoleum of Tresendar Manor
skeletons attack any creature that comes within 10 feet of the doors unless wearing a Redbrand cloak or speaking the password 'Illefarn'
skeletons guard the doors leading to areas 5 and 6 in the mausoleum
Two Redbrand ruffians stand guard in the manor, taunting prisoners and preparing to surprise intruders
can be used by players to pick the simple locks on cell doors with a successful DC 10 Dexterity check
The players are exploring the north barracks area of the cavern
lurks near the west ends of the two bridges in the cave beneath Tresendar Manor
serves as guardian of the cave, watching for intruders and hiding behind stone columns
uses its Weird Insight ability to discern characters' secrets when it notices intruders
three bugbears are present in the south barracks, bullying their goblin slave
one goblin named Droop is present in the south barracks, falls unconscious at the sight of the party
if the players are not wearing scarlet cloaks or fail deception, the bugbears will attack the party
if players wear scarlet cloaks and succeed on a 15 Charisma (Deception) check, they can persuade bugbears to help deal with 'traitors' or 'impostors'
The players have suddenly appeared in the south barracks, causing the goblin to faint
The party is fighting bugbears in the south barracks and will receive 600 XP if they defeat them
Four Redbrand ruffians are drinking and playing knucklebones in this headquarters and meeting room
The knucklebones game is close to turning acrimonious, with loaded dice and heavy drinking creating tension
If characters burst into the room, they automatically surprise the Redbrand ruffians
mentioned in Urmon's journal as being lost when Wave Echo Cave vanished, with information about it found in the wizards_quarters
Iarno's apparatus is set up to brew this potion in his quarters, though he hasn't succeeded yet
grabs his staff of defense from his chest when warned of approaching trouble
quaffs the potion of invisibility from his satchel when pursued by characters
Iarno uses his staff of defense to cast offensive spells at the players when threatened
strangers are due to arrive in Phandalin according to spies in Neverwinter
Lord Albrek instructs Iarno to capture the strangers if possible
Lord Albrek instructs Iarno to kill the strangers if necessary
the strangers may have dwarven maps in their possession
Iarno wields the staff of defense in Tresendar Manor
Sildar Hallwinter offers double XP reward if players capture Iarno Albrek and deliver him to Sildar in Phandalin
is being held at Cragmaw Castle according to Sildar Hallwinter's quest
can be encountered on the Triboar Trail during day or night encounters
can be encountered on the Triboar Trail during day encounters
can be encountered on the Triboar Trail during day or night encounters
can be encountered on the Triboar Trail during day or night encounters
can be encountered on the Triboar Trail during day or night encounters
can be encountered on the Triboar Trail during day or night encounters
can be encountered on the Triboar Trail during day or night encounters
can be encountered on the Triboar Trail during night encounters
travel along the Triboar Trail, potentially encountering dangerous creatures
The band of scouts based at Wyvern Tor ambushes travelers and burns homesteads as they roam
The characters enter the ruins of Conyberry to locate Agatha's lair
If the characters exercise caution, they will be able to speak with the banshee Agatha
The players are present in Conyberry when the spectral elf appears
Kost controls the zombies as guardians and can call them off during combat
twelve zombies lurk inside the crumbled watchtower to keep intruders out
Hamun Kost has set up a campsite at the ruins and is exploring the site for arcane lore
Kost offers to provide information if players remove orcs at Wyvern Tor or ask Agatha the banshee about the tower's builder
found in a leather bag inside a chest in Hamun Kost's tent at Old Owl Well
found in a bone tube inside a leather bag in a chest in Hamun Kost's tent at Old Owl Well
found in a tiny jeweled box inside a leather bag in a chest in Hamun Kost's tent at Old Owl Well
The party approaches the ruins of Thundertree as described in the narrative
The players are exploring the abandoned settlement of Thundertree, encountering dilapidated buildings and ominous warnings
two twig blights hide among the weeds flanking the cottage's open doorway
the blights will attack in self-defense or come to the aid of other twig blights if combat erupts
additional twig blights join the fray one round after the initial attack
pursues any characters they see, attacking until destroyed when living creatures enter the building
lurks in the shadows of the building, groaning and stirring when living creatures enter
The party helps Reidoth by chasing off Venomfang from Thundertree
Two giant spiders hide on the inner walls of the ruined storeroom
Spiders sense prey moving through webs and attack, surprising characters with passive Perception less than 17
Players are exploring the ruined storeroom where webs fill two squares north of the doorway and square 6, creating difficult terrain and potential restraint hazards
worn by the shriveled elf corpse cocooned in spider silk in the western half of the building
found in a belt pouch on the shriveled elf corpse during a careful search
Venomfang is the name of the young green dragon mentioned in the text
Venomfang has claimed the tower in Thundertree as its lair
Venomfang is laying low in the tower and does not want to give up the lair
part of the treasure in Venomfang's hoard that the dragon admires
part of the treasure in Venomfang's hoard that the dragon admires
spends time greedily admiring the loot including this scroll
spends time greedily admiring the loot including this scroll
Five ash zombies lurk within the barracks building in Thundertree
The party is present in the old guardroom where they can destroy ash zombies
Six human cultists are hiding in a cottage at Thundertree, with four standing guard and two resting
Four cultists stand guard (two in each room) while the other two rest in the larger chamber
Four cultists stand guard inside the cottage, with doors and shutters barred from inside
orc sentry guards the hidden camp at Wyvern Tor
Gog the ogre lives in the cave at Wyvern Tor
Brughor Axe-Biter (orc with 30 hit points) commands the other orcs
orc marauders are allied with Gog the ogre in the cave
lone orc sentry guards the camp entrance at Wyvern Tor
characters fight Brughor Axe-Biter and the orc marauders
characters fight Gog the ogre who fights until slain
Players ascend a rugged ridge and observe the cave entrance from fifty yards away
Players are at Wyvern Tor where they can find treasure in the cave
characters might try to talk their way inside the castle
posted as sentries by King Grol to drive off intruders who get too close to the castle
Three goblins bunk in the north barracks, making use of every bit of available space
Goblins inhabit the collapsed barracks, though the rubble appears dangerous, the tower is stable
contains a small cask of exceptional dwarven brandy that can restore 1 hit point per glass, but poisons characters who drink two glasses within an hour
Four hobgoblins are quartered in this room
They are quick to defend their tower if any intruders appear
Seven miserable goblins inhabit the foul mess hall in Cragmaw Castle
Yegg (a fat, cantankerous goblin with 12 hit points) viciously bullies and commands his unwilling goblin assistants as chief cook
The grick lives in a chamber within Cragmaw Castle, climbing up to a hidden ledge in the shadows to observe intruders before attacking.
The grick quietly observes intruders (players) who enter its chamber, then drops down to strike, potentially surprising them based on Stealth vs Perception checks.
Players are exploring the wizard's quarters where the statuette is hidden
Two ordinary goblins serve as acolytes to Lhupo, the goblin with 12 hit points
All three goblins kneel before the altar praying to Maglubiyet, the goblin god
can be used to open the locked iron door at the side entrance to the castle

A character who succeeds on a 15 Wisdom (Perception) check spots a footpath leading up to the hidden entrance. If actively searching the outside of the castle for a hidden entrance, they can make a 10 Wisdom (Perception) check to spot the canvas 'door'.
Two hobgoblins stand guard in this room and keep watch from the arrow slit
The Cragmaws have captured an owlbear and confined it to this tower room
If a character throws it fresh meat, the owlbear devours the food. Otherwise, it attacks the first creature it sees in the doorway
contained within the battered wooden chest on the jagged ledge of the tower's second floor
contained within the battered wooden chest on the jagged ledge of the tower's second floor
contained within the battered wooden chest on the jagged ledge of the tower's second floor
King Grol rules the Cragmaws from Cragmaw Castle through intimidation
Snarl the wolf attends King Grol as his companion
Vyerith the doppelganger is negotiating with King Grol over payment for Gundren and the map
Vyerith intends to question then kill Gundren Rockseeker
King Grol holds Gundren hostage and is ready to kill him if the players don't back off
If warned of an attack, Vyerith hides behind the door to ambush intruders from the rear
King Grol threatens to kill Gundren if the players don't back off
Gundren Rockseeker is described as a dwarf commoner, indicating his social status and role
three potions of healing are hidden under Grol's bed mattress in a stitched leather sack at Cragmaw Castle
Targor Bloodsword leads the war band of three hobgoblins
Targor Bloodsword has two wolves as pets that accompany the war band
Targor has long aspired to lead the Cragmaw tribe and could be persuaded to see himself as Grol's successor rather than avenger
players know the location of Wave Echo Cave and must seek out the old dwarven delve to discover the Black Spider's identity and motives
The adventurers are exploring this section of Wave Echo Cave, which is designed for characters of at least 4th level with 2,700+ XP
can be encountered as wandering monsters in the cave
can be encountered as wandering monsters in the cave
can be encountered as wandering monsters in the cave
can be encountered as wandering monsters in the cave
can be encountered as wandering monsters in the cave
can be encountered as wandering monsters in the cave
can be encountered as wandering monsters in the cave
can attack players as wandering monsters in the cave
can attack players as wandering monsters in the cave
can attack players as wandering monsters in the cave
can attack players as wandering monsters in the cave
The rhythmic thunder of pounding waves in the cave piques players' curiosity and draws them deeper into the mine toward its source
Players can climb the pit walls, with failure potentially causing them to fall to the bottom
Nezznar overlooked the boots in his haste to explore the rest of Wave Echo Cave
patiently lurking in one of the dead-end passages of the old mine section
Clinging to the ceiling like bats in the old entrance tunnel
nine skeletons form in this chamber when any living creature enters and fight until destroyed
can find faint Dwarvish markings on disintegrating paper scraps in cubbyholes that record weigh-ins and disbursements
The players are exploring this chamber that was once an office or storeroom
required to open the locked iron strongbox behind the counter
Three ghouls from the pack are in the south barracks, cracking and gnawing on ancient bones
poisonous gas released by fungal carpets forces players to make Constitution saving throws or take poison damage and become poisoned
Seven ghouls lurk in the shadows on the western ledge of the great cavern
Characters can explore the pool and find an old skeleton on the bottom
clutched in the bony fingers of a skeleton at the dark pool
Five bugbears reside in this chamber, which was formerly a barracks for miners
Bugbears are here to prevent ghouls, zombies, or other undead from troubling their master in his lair
Eight zombie dwarf warriors rise and pursue living creatures that enter the room
Intelligent undead creature guards this area to prevent intruders from passing through
Acts on ancient instructions to prevent intruders (players) from passing through
Characters can sense a subtle aura of magic in the cavern, which becomes stronger as they approach the northern building
spends his time here because his treasure is in the scorched chest
rises up out of the floor when a living creature enters the room
summoned to guard the magic items created and stored in the Forge of Spells
attacks the party if they attempt to remove anything from the area
can trick the spectator into thinking they are wizards or miners sent to terminate its employment, causing it to return to its home plane
The players are present in the forge of spells, being addressed by the spherical creature
can be temporarily created by bathing nonmagical weapons in the brazier's green flame at the Forge of Spells
can be temporarily created by bathing nonmagical armor in the brazier's green flame at the Forge of Spells
located on the worktable in the southeast corner of the forge of spells
located on the worktable in the southeast corner of the forge of spells
The old streambed remains as a usable passage that circumvents the undead in 12, although Nezznar has not yet discovered this
Three bugbears are stationed in the collapsed cavern
Two bugbears are clearing rock on the rift floor
One bugbear stands guard in the western half of the cavern
Two bugbears guard the rift floor while clearing rubble
Doppelganger named Vhalak supervises operations in the cavern
Vhalak supervises the bugbears' operation in the guise of a male drow
Players can be present in the rift location and interact with its features
buried under heavy rubble at the bottom of the rift, worn by a crushed dwarf skeleton
can attempt a Wisdom (Perception) check to find the hidden gauntlets, one check per hour per character
uses this room as his headquarters while exploring the mines and searching for the Forge of Spells
four giant spiders defend their master to the death
spiders hide behind pillars and try to entangle intruders in webs before closing to melee range
assumes the guise of Nundro Rockseeker so Nezznar can use the 'dwarf' as leverage to force the party's surrender
retreated to this area and works with Nezznar to deceive the party
have established a campsite with bedrolls, packs, and a fire pit in the temple hall
Sildar Hallwinter is a representative of the Lords' Alliance who can escort Nezznar to Neverwinter
Nezznar carries a potion of healing in the temple quarters
Nezznar carries a spider staff in the temple quarters
Nundro Rockseeker is described as a dwarf commoner, indicating his social status
Nundro Rockseeker is lying on the floor of this cell where he is being held captive
The door to this room requires thieves' tools and a successful Dexterity check to open
Nundro offers to join the players' party if they rescue him from his cell
serves as a safe resting place for the players after dealing with Nezznar and his minions
Players are tasked with rescuing Nundro from his cell in the priests' quarters
takes over administration of the new mine at Wave Echo Cave
awards the party a 10 percent share of the mine's profits for their service to the family
Young Green Dragon is also known as Venomfang
